items.title: LIVE: Fanatics Super Bowl Party
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>AP is live at the pre-Super Bowl Fanatics party with NFL stars, Cardi B and other celebrities walking the red carpet at a San Francisco pier the day before the big game.

#superbowl #fanatics #cardib</article>

items.title: Protesters clash with police near Winter Olympics venue in Milan
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Italian police fired tear gas and a water cannon at dozens of protesters who threw firecrackers and tried to access a highway near a Winter Olympics venue on Saturday.

items.title: Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner visit USS Abraham Lincoln after Iran talks
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>U.S. Mideast special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner visited the USS Abraham Lincoln in the Arabian Sea on Saturday, along with the head of U.S. Central Command Adm. Brad Cooper, after meeting with Iranian officials in Oman on Friday.

items.content:
<article>The Milan Cortina Winter Olympics have begun with 16 sports, some never seen before. Here's a guide to everything you need to know.

items.title: Protesters in Bangladesh seek justice for activist killed in December
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Clashes erupted in Bangladesh's capital on Friday between police and protesters as demonstrators took to the streets over the death of student activist Sharif Osman Hadi in December.

#bangladesh

items.title: What is A-fib and how is it treated?
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Atrial fibrillation, or A-fib, is becoming more common, but doctors say treatments are improving. The condition is a quivering or irregular heartbeat that can lead to blood clots, stroke and heart failure. Smartwatches and other devices are helping with diagnosis.</article>

items.title: AP recaps scene at San Siro Stadium during Winter Olympics opening ceremony
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Associated Press journalists joined thousands of spectators heading into Milan’s San Siro Stadium to cover the opening ceremony of the Milan Cortina Winter Olympics, as Italy welcomed the Games back for the first time in 20 years.

items.title: Summer Olympians say they're ready for the Winter Games. As fans
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>While many summer Olympians are setting their sights on the 2028 Los Angeles Games, some are first prioritizing this month's Milan Cortina Winter Games... as fans.</article>

items.title: Jaxson Dart, Baker Mayfield and more join On Football Super Bowl edition
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Damar Hamlin, Jaxson Dart, Cam Skattebo, Barry Sanders and Baker Mayfield join On Football with Rob Maaddi from San Francisco ahead of Super Bowl LX.</article>

items.title: Yvette Clarke reacts to Trump post depicting Barack and Michelle Obama as primates
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A post on President Donald Trump's social media account depicting former President Barack Obama and his wife, Michelle Obama, as primates in a jungle has been taken down. The Associated Press spoke with New York Congresswoman Yvette Clarke about her reactions. (AP Video by River Zhang).

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Thousands attend funeral in Pakistan after deadly suicide bombing killed 31
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Burials took place Saturday for victims of a suicide bombing a day earlier inside a Shiite mosque on the outskirts of Pakistan’s capital. Thirty-one people were killed and 169 others wounded in the attack that an affiliate of the Islamic State group claimed responsibility for overnight. (AP Video shot by Muhammad Yousaf).

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Immigrant whose skull was broken during ICE arrest says beating was unprovoked
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Alberto Castañeda Mondragón, a construction worker in Minnesota, talks about his arrest by ICE officers which resulted in eight skull fractures and five life-threatening brain hemorrhages. (AP Video: Mark Vancleave).
